invariant_definition: Non-existent in the strictest sense — what cannot be the object of any cognition because it has no being whatever (the *vandhyā-suta*, son of a barren woman; the *gagana-kusuma*, sky-flower; the *śaśa-śṛṅga*, hare's horn). All Vedāntic schools agree on the standard examples and on *asat*'s strict meaning. The crucial Advaita point: the world is NOT *asat*; *mithyā* ≠ *asat*. The conflation of *mithyā* and *asat* is the canonical mistranslation the user's project is concerned to undo.

per_school.definition: *Asat* is sheer non-existence; the world is precisely NOT *asat* (it has *vyāvahārika* reality and is sublatable). The world is *mithyā* — *anirvacanīya*, neither *sat* nor *asat*.

Standard examples accepted; the polemical use is to insist on the *sat-asat* binary against the Advaita *anirvacanīya* third option. The Mādhva position: the world is *sat* (real, dependent); there is no third ontological status.

translator_note: 'Non-existent' is the standard gloss. The user's position-brief explicitly forbids the conflation *mithyā* = *asat* — see *mithya* entry.
